page_content:
SOCY391 Surveillance and the Erosion of Privacy (3 Credits)

Our world is under increasing amounts of surveillance, from states, corporations, and other individuals. These activities may affect our sense of privacy since surveilling others is done as a means to gather knowledge. This class examines surveillance and privacy together

in order to answer the following questions: Are acts of surveillance

in contemporary society eroding our sense of privacy? Are certain populations more or less privileged in terms of having privacy rights? How can we protect privacy rights in an age of surveillance?
